title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: cdiscmask R package anonymizing CDISC clinical trial data (SDTM ADaM). fully implemented source, tests, vignette.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Anonymize CDISC-standard datasets (SDTM, ADaM) preserving data types (e.g., AGE stays numeric, bucketed) Handle cross-domain consistency: date shifts subject ID must identical across datasets Support reversible anonymization — track transformations original data can recovered Auto-detect sensitive fields (subject IDs, dates, etc.) leave non-sensitive fields (units, normal ranges, labs) unchanged Intended end-use: feed anonymized CDISC data agentic workflows without exposing PII

title: CLAUDE.md
text: project .Rprofile calls tinytex::use_tinytex() put pdflatex PATH R CMD check. PDF manual build still fails, use devtools::check(args = "---manual"). Tests use library(safetyData) real CDISC datasets (sdtm_dm, sdtm_ae, sdtm_lb, adam_adsl).
code: devtools::load_all()                                  # load package interactively devtools::test()                                      # run all tests devtools::check()                                     # full R CMD check devtools::check(args = "--no-manual")                 # skip PDF manual (if pdflatex unavailable) devtools::document()                                  # regenerate NAMESPACE + man/ from roxygen testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-mask.R")    # run a single test file

title: CLAUDE.md
text: public functions funnel shared cdiscmask_key object (created new_mask_key()) stores every per-subject mapping. Passing key multiple mask_domain() calls guarantees cross-domain consistency — central design invariant. masking order mask_domain() load-bearing: dates shifted first (keyed original subject ID), subject IDs replaced, free text. unmask_domain() reverses opposite order.
code: mask_cdisc(domains, key)          # multi-domain entry point   └─ mask_domain(df, key)         # per-domain orchestrator        ├─ detect_sensitive_cols() # classify columns by type        ├─ shift_dates()           # shift dates while original IDs are still in the df        ├─ mask_subject_id()       # pseudonymize IDs, write to key        └─ mask_free_text()        # redact verbatim text, write originals to key  unmask_domain(df, key)            # reverse in opposite order: free_text → IDs → dates save_mask_key() / load_mask_key() # persist key to .rds

title: CLAUDE.md
text: cdiscmask_key environment, list — means mask_* functions mutate place rather returning new copy. Callers share state reference. "cdiscmask_cols" attribute — mask_domain() stores column classification attr(df, "cdiscmask_cols") returned data frame. unmask_domain() requires attribute; errors absent. Numeric types preserved — AGE similar numeric variables never converted categories. subject IDs (character) dates transformed. Partial ISO-8601 dates ("2020-03", "2020") CDISC "--" unknown-date placeholders left untouched .shift_dtc() — full-precision YYYY-MM-DD strings (optional time component) shifted. Day-offset columns (CDISC suffix DY, e.g. AEDY) plain integers shifted arithmetically per-subject day offset — date parsing. Free text category-preserving — verbatim/narrative columns categorical columns (ARM, TRT, STUDYID) get opaque labels like AETERM-0001 preserve category structure. original → label map stored key$free_text[[col]]. Column detection rules detect_sensitive_cols(): subject_id: exact names USUBJID, SUBJID dates: columns matching DTC$|DTM$ (SDTM) Date-class columns (ADaM) dy: columns matching DY$, excluding already classified dates free_text: suffix match TERM$|TRT$|NAM$|TEXT$|VERBATIM$; STUDYID; character columns matching ^TRT|^ARM|^ACTARM (numeric/date variants excluded)

title: Getting started with cdiscmask
text: state lives single cdiscmask_key object. Creating one first step every workflow. Passing key every masking call guarantees subject mapped SUBJ-0001 DM also SUBJ-0001 AE LB, dates shifted exactly number days.
code: key <- new_mask_key() key #> <cdiscmask_key> #>   0 subject ID mapping(s) #>   0 date offset(s) #>   0 free-text column(s) stored

title: Getting started with cdiscmask
text: mask_domain() detects sensitive columns automatically applies right strategy type: Notice AGE, SEX, RACE untouched — identifiable information transformed. Dates shift random number days preserving date format, USUBJID SUBJID receive pseudonyms.
code: dm_masked <- mask_domain(sdtm_dm, key, domain = "DM")  dm_masked[1:4, c("USUBJID", "SUBJID", "RFSTDTC", "RFENDTC", "AGE", "SEX", "RACE")] #>     USUBJID    SUBJID    RFSTDTC    RFENDTC AGE SEX  RACE #> 1 SUBJ-0001 SUBJ-0307 2014-07-16 2015-01-13  63   F WHITE #> 2 SUBJ-0002 SUBJ-0308 2012-06-21 2012-07-19  64   M WHITE #> 3 SUBJ-0003 SUBJ-0309 2012-12-18 2013-06-15  71   M WHITE #> 4 SUBJ-0004 SUBJ-0310 2013-05-30 2013-06-26  74   M WHITE

what: Masking multiple domains with a shared key
title: Getting started with cdiscmask
text: key point cross-domain design: calling mask_domain() key additional domains reuses existing pseudonyms date offsets. can verify subject pseudonym three domains: Date consistency can confirmed checking offset identical across domains:
code:
ae_masked <- mask_domain(sdtm_ae, key, domain = "AE") lb_masked <- mask_domain(sdtm_lb, key, domain = "LB") orig_subj <- "01-701-1015" pseudonym <- key$id_map[orig_subj]  cat("Pseudonym assigned:", pseudonym, "
") #> Pseudonym assigned: SUBJ-0001 cat("Present in DM:", pseudonym %in% dm_masked$USUBJID, "
") #> Present in DM: TRUE cat("Present in AE:", pseudonym %in% ae_masked$USUBJID, "
") #> Present in AE: TRUE cat("Present in LB:", pseudonym %in% lb_masked$USUBJID, "
") #> Present in LB: TRUE # The date shift for this subject (in days) offset <- key$date_offsets[orig_subj] cat("Date offset for", orig_subj, ":", offset, "days

") #> Date offset for 01-701-1015 : 195 days  # Original vs masked reference start date in DM cat("DM RFSTDTC original:", sdtm_dm$RFSTDTC[sdtm_dm$USUBJID == orig_subj][1], "
") #> DM RFSTDTC original: 2014-01-02 cat("DM RFSTDTC masked:  ", dm_masked$RFSTDTC[dm_masked$USUBJID == pseudonym][1], "

") #> DM RFSTDTC masked:   2014-07-16  # Original vs masked AE date — shifted by the same offset ae_orig <- sdtm_ae[sdtm_ae$USUBJID == orig_subj, ] ae_mask <- ae_masked[ae_masked$USUBJID == pseudonym, ] cat("AE AESTDTC original:", ae_orig$AESTDTC[1], "
") #> AE AESTDTC original: 2014-01-03 cat("AE AESTDTC masked:  ", ae_mask$AESTDTC[1], "
") #> AE AESTDTC masked:   2014-07-17

title: Getting started with cdiscmask
text: named list domains, mask_cdisc() handles key creation loops domains one call.
code: masked <- mask_cdisc(list(   DM = sdtm_dm,   AE = sdtm_ae,   LB = sdtm_lb,   VS = sdtm_vs ))  # The key is attached as an attribute key2 <- attr(masked, "key") key2 #> <cdiscmask_key> #>   612 subject ID mapping(s) #>   306 date offset(s) #>   6 free-text column(s) stored # AE adverse event term is redacted; other columns are preserved masked$AE[1:3, c("USUBJID", "AETERM", "AESTDTC", "AESTDY", "AESEV", "AESER")] #>     USUBJID      AETERM    AESTDTC AESTDY AESEV AESER #> 1 SUBJ-0001 AETERM-0001 2013-03-16   -291  MILD     N #> 2 SUBJ-0001 AETERM-0002 2013-03-16   -291  MILD     N #> 3 SUBJ-0001 AETERM-0003 2013-03-22   -285  MILD     N

title: Getting started with cdiscmask
text: mask_cdisc() works identically ADaM. Date-class columns (e.g. TRTSDT) detected automatically.
code: adam_masked <- mask_cdisc(list(   ADSL = adam_adsl,   ADAE = adam_adae ))  adam_masked$ADSL[1:3, c("USUBJID", "TRTSDT", "TRTEDT", "AGE", "SEX", "ARM")] #>     USUBJID     TRTSDT     TRTEDT AGE SEX      ARM #> 1 SUBJ-0001 2013-02-27 2013-08-27  63   F ARM-0001 #> 2 SUBJ-0002 2013-07-30 2013-08-26  64   M ARM-0001 #> 3 SUBJ-0003 2013-03-07 2013-09-02  71   M ARM-0002

title: Getting started with cdiscmask
text: unmask_domain() restores original data exactly. cdiscmask_cols attribute mask_domain() attaches masked data frame tells unmask_domain() columns restore .
code: dm_back <- unmask_domain(masked$DM, attr(masked, "key"))  # Every column is identical to the original identical(dm_back$USUBJID,  sdtm_dm$USUBJID) #> [1] TRUE identical(dm_back$RFSTDTC,  sdtm_dm$RFSTDTC) #> [1] TRUE identical(dm_back$AGE,      sdtm_dm$AGE) #> [1] TRUE ae_back <- unmask_domain(masked$AE, attr(masked, "key"))  identical(ae_back$AETERM,   sdtm_ae$AETERM) #> [1] TRUE identical(ae_back$AESTDTC,  sdtm_ae$AESTDTC) #> [1] TRUE identical(ae_back$AESTDY,   sdtm_ae$AESTDY) #> [1] TRUE

title: Getting started with cdiscmask
text: share masked datasets collaborator later restore originals, save key separately data. key must stored transmitted securely — artefact links pseudonyms back real subject identifiers.
code: save_mask_key(key2, "masking_key.rds")  # Later, or in a different session: key_loaded <- load_mask_key("masking_key.rds") dm_restored <- unmask_domain(masked$DM, key_loaded)

title: Getting started with cdiscmask
text: automatic detection misses column picks one shouldn’t, pass custom cols list mask_domain():
code: my_cols <- detect_sensitive_cols(sdtm_ae) my_cols$free_text <- c(my_cols$free_text, "AEOUT")  # also redact outcome text  ae_custom <- mask_domain(sdtm_ae, key, cols = my_cols)

title: Getting started with cdiscmask
text: core workflow three steps:
code: # 1. Create (or load) a key key <- new_mask_key()  # 2. Mask — reuse the same key for every domain masked <- mask_cdisc(list(DM = sdtm_dm, AE = sdtm_ae, LB = sdtm_lb), key = key)  # 3. Save the key securely so you can unmask later save_mask_key(key, "masking_key.rds")

code: @Manual{,   title = {cdiscmask: Anonymize CDISC Clinical Trial Data},   author = {Richard Hooijmaijers},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://richardhooijmaijers.github.io/cdiscmask}, }

title: cdiscmask · Anonymise CDISC clinical-trial data
text: cdiscmask R package anonymizing CDISC clinical trial data (SDTM ADaM). replaces subject identifiers pseudonyms, shifts dates per-subject random offset, redacts verbatim/categorical text — leaving non-sensitive columns (AGE, SEX, lab units, normal ranges) completely unchanged. Key properties: Cross-domain consistency — subject ID date shift applied identically across domains shared masking key used. Reversible — every transformation recorded masking key original data can recovered exactly. Type-preserving — numeric columns stay numeric; character columns stay character; Date-class columns stay Date. Auto-detection — sensitive columns identified CDISC naming conventions (manual column lists required).

title: cdiscmask · Anonymise CDISC clinical-trial data
text: package yet CRAN. Install GitHub:
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("RichardHooijmaijers/cdiscmask")

title: cdiscmask · Anonymise CDISC clinical-trial data
code: library(cdiscmask) library(safetyData)  # provides sdtm_dm, sdtm_ae, sdtm_lb, adam_adsl, ...  # Mask multiple domains with a single call — one shared key guarantees consistency masked <- mask_cdisc(list(   DM = sdtm_dm,   AE = sdtm_ae,   LB = sdtm_lb ))  # Inspect: AGE/SEX untouched, USUBJID pseudonymised, dates shifted masked$DM[1:3, c("USUBJID", "RFSTDTC", "AGE", "SEX", "ARM")]  # Recover original data using the attached key key <- attr(masked, "key") dm_original <- unmask_domain(masked$DM, key)  # Persist the key so you can unmask in a later session save_mask_key(key, "masking_key.rds") key <- load_mask_key("masking_key.rds")

title: cdiscmask · Anonymise CDISC clinical-trial data
text: masking state lives cdiscmask_key object. Pass key every mask_domain() call package guarantees: subject pseudonymised SUBJ-0042 DM SUBJ-0042 every domain. Dates subject shifted exactly number days everywhere.
code: key <- new_mask_key()  dm_masked <- mask_domain(sdtm_dm, key, domain = "DM") ae_masked <- mask_domain(sdtm_ae, key, domain = "AE")  # reuses mappings from above

what: cdiscmask: Anonymize CDISC Clinical Trial Data — cdiscmask-package
title: cdiscmask: Anonymize CDISC Clinical Trial Data — cdiscmask-package
text: Anonymizes CDISC-standard datasets (SDTM, ADaM) preserving data types cross-domain consistency. Subject identifiers replaced pseudonyms, dates shifted per-subject random offset applied uniformly across domains, reversible key stored original data can recovered. Sensitive columns detected automatically based CDISC variable naming conventions.

title: cdiscmask: Anonymize CDISC Clinical Trial Data — cdiscmask-package

what: Detect sensitive columns in a CDISC dataset — detect_sensitive_cols
title: Detect sensitive columns in a CDISC dataset — detect_sensitive_cols
text: Identifies columns anonymized based CDISC variable naming conventions column classes. Returns separate vectors sensitivity category callers can apply different masking strategies per type.

title: Detect sensitive columns in a CDISC dataset — detect_sensitive_cols
code: detect_sensitive_cols(df, domain = NULL)

title: Detect sensitive columns in a CDISC dataset — detect_sensitive_cols
text: df data frame representing one CDISC domain. domain Optional domain name (e.g. "DM", "AE"). Reserved future domain-specific rules; currently unused.

title: Detect sensitive columns in a CDISC dataset — detect_sensitive_cols
text: named list elements: subject_id: row-level subject identifiers (USUBJID, SUBJID) dates: character DTC/DTM columns Date-class DT columns dy: integer study-day columns (suffix DY) free_text: verbatim / narrative / categorical columns need category-preserving masking: verbatim terms (TERM, TRT, NAM, TEXT, VERBATIM suffixes), study identifier (STUDYID), character treatment / arm assignment columns (ARM*, ACTARM*, character TRT* columns) : reserved future domain-specific flagging

title: Detect sensitive columns in a CDISC dataset — detect_sensitive_cols
code: df <- data.frame(   USUBJID = "01-001",   RFSTDTC = "2020-01-15",   AGE     = 45L,   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) detect_sensitive_cols(df) #> $subject_id #> [1] "USUBJID" #>  #> $dates #> [1] "RFSTDTC" #>  #> $dy #> character(0) #>  #> $free_text #> character(0) #>  #> $other #> character(0) #>

title: Load a masking key from disk — load_mask_key
code: key <- new_mask_key() tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".rds") save_mask_key(key, tmp) key2 <- load_mask_key(tmp) identical(key$id_map, key2$id_map) #> [1] TRUE

title: Mask multiple CDISC domains consistently — mask_cdisc
text: domains named list data frames, one per CDISC domain (e.g. list(DM = dm_df, AE = ae_df, LB = lb_df)). key cdiscmask_key. NULL new key created automatically.

title: Mask multiple CDISC domains consistently — mask_cdisc
text: list names domains, element masked data frame. key used attached attribute "key".

title: Mask multiple CDISC domains consistently — mask_cdisc
code: if (requireNamespace("safetyData", quietly = TRUE)) {   masked <- mask_cdisc(list(     DM = safetyData::sdtm_dm,     AE = safetyData::sdtm_ae   ))   attr(masked, "key") } #> <cdiscmask_key> #>   612 subject ID mapping(s) #>   306 date offset(s) #>   6 free-text column(s) stored

what: Mask a single CDISC domain data frame — mask_domain
title: Mask a single CDISC domain data frame — mask_domain
text: Applies anonymization preserving column types recording every transformation key unmask_domain() can reverse . key reused across multiple domains, subject-ID pseudonyms date shifts guaranteed consistent.

title: Mask a single CDISC domain data frame — mask_domain
code: mask_domain(df, key, domain = NULL, cols = NULL)

title: Mask a single CDISC domain data frame — mask_domain
text: df data frame one CDISC domain. key cdiscmask_key created new_mask_key(). domain Optional domain name passed detect_sensitive_cols(). cols optional named list overriding automatic detection; structure return value detect_sensitive_cols().

title: Mask a single CDISC domain data frame — mask_domain
text: data frame sensitive values replaced. attribute "cdiscmask_cols" records columns masked.

title: Mask a single CDISC domain data frame — mask_domain
text: masking order : Date columns shifted (keyed original subject ID). Subject-ID columns replaced pseudonyms. Free-text columns redacted. original column classification stored "cdiscmask_cols" attribute returned data frame unmask_domain() knows restore.

title: Mask a single CDISC domain data frame — mask_domain
code: if (requireNamespace("safetyData", quietly = TRUE)) {   key       <- new_mask_key()   dm_masked <- mask_domain(safetyData::sdtm_dm, key, domain = "DM")   dm_masked[1:3, c("USUBJID", "RFSTDTC", "AGE", "SEX")] } #>     USUBJID    RFSTDTC AGE SEX #> 1 SUBJ-0001 2013-11-13  63   F #> 2 SUBJ-0002 2011-09-01  64   M #> 3 SUBJ-0003 2013-01-04  71   M

what: Mask free-text / verbatim / categorical columns — mask_free_text
title: Mask free-text / verbatim / categorical columns — mask_free_text
text: Replaces unique value opaque label preserves category structure (e.g. rows "DIARRHOEA" get label, counts within-subject comparisons remain valid). Labels formed <prefix>-XXXX prefix defaults column name.

title: Mask free-text / verbatim / categorical columns — mask_free_text
code: mask_free_text(df, cols, key, prefix = NULL)

title: Mask free-text / verbatim / categorical columns — mask_free_text
text: df data frame. cols Character vector column names mask. key cdiscmask_key created new_mask_key(). prefix Named character vector mapping column names label prefixes, single string used every column. Defaults column name, giving labels like AETERM-0001, ARM-0001, STUDYID-0001.

title: Mask free-text / verbatim / categorical columns — mask_free_text
text: df cols replaced opaque labels. key updated place original--label mapping column.

title: Mask free-text / verbatim / categorical columns — mask_free_text
text: column masked later call (e.g. second domain containing column), existing mappings reused original value always receives label. mapping (original → label) stored key$free_text[[col]] unmask_domain() can reverse .

title: Mask free-text / verbatim / categorical columns — mask_free_text
code: key <- new_mask_key() df  <- data.frame(AETERM = c("HEADACHE", "NAUSEA", "HEADACHE"),                   stringsAsFactors = FALSE) mask_free_text(df, cols = "AETERM", key = key) #>        AETERM #> 1 AETERM-0001 #> 2 AETERM-0002 #> 3 AETERM-0001

title: Replace subject identifiers with pseudonyms — mask_subject_id

text: df col replaced pseudonyms. key updated place.

title: Replace subject identifiers with pseudonyms — mask_subject_id
code: key <- new_mask_key() df  <- data.frame(USUBJID = c("01-001", "01-002", "01-001"),                   stringsAsFactors = FALSE) mask_subject_id(df, col = "USUBJID", key = key) #>     USUBJID #> 1 SUBJ-0001 #> 2 SUBJ-0002 #> 3 SUBJ-0001

what: Shift date/datetime columns by a per-subject random offset — shift_dates
title: Shift date/datetime columns by a per-subject random offset — shift_dates
text: subject receives random integer offset (days) drawn stored key. offset reused across domains, preserving within-subject temporal relationships.

title: Shift date/datetime columns by a per-subject random offset — shift_dates
code: shift_dates(df, cols, subject_col = "USUBJID", key, range = c(-365L, 365L))

title: Shift date/datetime columns by a per-subject random offset — shift_dates
text: df data frame. cols Character vector column names shift. subject_col Name subject-ID column used look create per-subject offsets key (default "USUBJID"). key cdiscmask_key created new_mask_key(). range Integer vector length 2 giving inclusive range (days) offsets sampled (default c(-365L, 365L)).

title: Shift date/datetime columns by a per-subject random offset — shift_dates
text: df cols shifted. key updated place.

title: Shift date/datetime columns by a per-subject random offset — shift_dates
text: Three column types handled: Character DTC/DTM (SDTM): ISO-8601 strings. Full dates (YYYY-MM-DD, optionally followed time component) shifted; partial dates (YYYY-MM, YYYY) CDISC "--" placeholder left untouched. Date objects (ADaM): shifted adding offset. Integer/numeric DY (study days): shifted arithmetically without date parsing.

title: Shift date/datetime columns by a per-subject random offset — shift_dates
code: key <- new_mask_key() df  <- data.frame(   USUBJID = c("01-001", "01-001", "01-002"),   RFSTDTC = c("2020-01-15", "2020-06-01", "2019-11-20"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) shift_dates(df, cols = "RFSTDTC", key = key) #>   USUBJID    RFSTDTC #> 1  01-001 2019-08-14 #> 2  01-001 2019-12-30 #> 3  01-002 2019-07-19

what: Reverse anonymization for a single domain — unmask_domain
title: Reverse anonymization for a single domain — unmask_domain
text: Uses mappings stored key restore original values columns masked mask_domain() mask_cdisc(). data frame must carry "cdiscmask_cols" attribute set masking, row order must unchanged.

title: Reverse anonymization for a single domain — unmask_domain
code: unmask_domain(df, key)

title: Reverse anonymization for a single domain — unmask_domain
text: df masked data frame produced mask_domain() mask_cdisc(). key cdiscmask_key used masking.

title: Reverse anonymization for a single domain — unmask_domain
text: original (unmasked) data frame.

title: Reverse anonymization for a single domain — unmask_domain
code: if (requireNamespace("safetyData", quietly = TRUE)) {   key       <- new_mask_key()   dm_masked <- mask_domain(safetyData::sdtm_dm, key)   dm_back   <- unmask_domain(dm_masked, key)   identical(dm_back$USUBJID, safetyData::sdtm_dm$USUBJID) } #> [1] TRUE

title: cdiscmask 0.1.0
text: first version package
